name: Cotingidae
description: Cotingas are a diverse family of Neotropical passerines known for their elaborate plumage, courtship displays, and lekking behavior. The family includes some of the most spectacularly ornamented birds in the world.

children.name: Rupicola
children.description: Rupicola is a genus of spectacularly crested cotingas that perform elaborate courtship displays on traditional lek sites.

children.name: Rupicola rupicola
children.description: The Guianan cock-of-the-rock is a brilliant orange bird with a fan-shaped crest that covers its bill. It inhabits rocky gorges and foothill forests of the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Rupicola peruvianus
children.description: The Andean cock-of-the-rock is the national bird of Peru, with dazzling scarlet-orange plumage and a prominent crest. Males gather at communal leks where they perform elaborate hopping and wing-flapping displays.

children.name: Procnias
children.description: Procnias is a genus of cotingas known for their extraordinarily loud, bell-like calls that carry for great distances through the forest.

children.name: Procnias nudicollis
children.description: The bare-throated bellbird is entirely white with a bare blue throat patch, found in the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. Its loud, metallic call is one of the most distinctive sounds of the forest.

children.name: Lipaugus
children.description: Lipaugus is a genus of relatively plain-plumaged cotingas known for their powerful, far-carrying songs.

children.name: Lipaugus vociferans
children.description: The screaming piha is a drab grey bird with one of the loudest songs of any bird, audible for over a kilometer. It is a characteristic voice of Amazonian lowland forests.

children.name: Querula
children.description: Querula is a monotypic genus of cotinga with a unique purple throat patch in males.

children.name: Querula purpurata
children.description: The purple-throated fruitcrow is a glossy black bird with a brilliant purple-red throat patch, found across Central and Amazonian South America. It is highly social and often seen in small groups.

children.name: Cephalopterus
children.description: Cephalopterus is a genus of large cotingas with a distinctive umbrella-like crest and an inflatable chest wattle.

children.name: Cephalopterus ornatus
children.description: The Amazonian umbrellabird is a large, all-black bird with a massive umbrella-shaped crest and a long, feathered wattle on its chest. It inhabits riverine and floodplain forests of the Amazon basin.
